---
title: "00 · Kiselev — Geometry, Book II (Stereometry)"
subtitle: "Lines and planes, polyhedra, round solids, volumes and surface areas, regular polyhedra — the classical Russian Stereometry."
---

## What this tier is for

This is the **Kiselev *Stereometry*** track — Book II of *Геометрия*,
the natural continuation of Planimetry into three dimensions.
Stereometry is where the formal proof-discipline of Book I has to do
real work: parallelism in space is more delicate than in the plane,
and the volume formulas need an honest argument, not a hand-wave.

By the end of Stereometry you can:

- Reason about **lines and planes in space** — when they are parallel,
  when they intersect, when they are perpendicular.
- Work with **dihedral and polyhedral angles**.
- Prove and apply the properties of **prisms, pyramids, and the five
  regular polyhedra**.
- Compute **volumes** and **surface areas** of cylinders, cones, and
  spheres — and understand *why* the formulas are what they are
  (Cavalieri, the cone-cylinder-sphere relations Archimedes proved).
- Handle **cross-sections** of solids by planes — the route into
  integration.
- Recognise **similar solids** and the cubic/quadratic scaling that
  governs their volumes and surface areas.

## Source

Givental's English translation (free, authoritative):
[Kiselev's Geometry, Book II — Stereometry, Sumizdat](https://www.cimat.mx/ciencia_para_jovenes/bachillerato/libros/%5BKiselev,Givental%5DKiselev's_Geom2.pdf).
The PDF stays external; the pointers in each lesson cite Givental's
chapter and section numbers.

::: {.callout-warning}
**Citation, not transcription.** Givental's translation is
copyrighted. Each lesson **adapts** Kiselev's exposition and exercises
in its own words and points the learner at the source PDF for the
canonical proofs. Do not copy Givental's prose directly.
:::

## Lessons

The full Stereometry track, in book order:

1. [**01 · Lines and Planes in Space (Ch 1)**](01-lines-and-planes/index.qmd) —
   coplanar / parallel / skew positions; perpendicular line to a
   plane; dihedral angles; the three-perpendiculars theorem.
2. [**02 · Polyhedra: Prisms and Pyramids (Ch 2)**](02-polyhedra/index.qmd) —
   volume of a prism ($Ah$); volume of a pyramid
   ($\tfrac{1}{3}Ah$); Cavalieri's principle.
3. [**03 · Cylinder and Cone (Ch 3)**](03-cylinder-cone/index.qmd) —
   the lateral surface as an unrolled rectangle / sector;
   Archimedes' $3 : 2 : 1$ ratio.
4. [**04 · The Sphere (Ch 4)**](04-sphere/index.qmd) — Archimedes'
   theorems: surface $4\pi R^2$ via projection, volume
   $\tfrac{4}{3}\pi R^3$ via Cavalieri.
5. [**05 · Regular Polyhedra (Ch 5)**](05-regular-polyhedra/index.qmd) —
   Euler's formula; the five Platonic solids and the proof that no
   sixth exists; similarity scaling.

**Track complete.** Together with the nine Planimetry chapters,
this is the full Kiselev geometry track in Russian-classical style.

## Sister synthesis tiers

The synthesis lessons covering the same ground sit in
[Tier 1 Foundations](../tier-1-foundations/index.qmd) (area, perimeter,
volume — the *Touch* layer) and the CBSE
[Class 9](../tier-cbse-09-class-9/index.qmd) and
[Class 10](../tier-cbse-10-class-10/index.qmd) tracks
(surface areas and volumes, mensuration). For the proof-heavy treatment
of three-dimensional geometry as a coordinate problem, see
[Tier 4](../tier-4-precalc-calculus/index.qmd) (vectors in 3D) and
[CBSE Class 12](../tier-cbse-12-class-12/index.qmd) (lines and planes
in 3D).
